name: Oregon (United States, reverse)

colors.symbolism: one of the two state colours
one of the two state colours

description: The reverse of the Oregon state flag: a navy-blue field charged with a single gold North American beaver (the state animal) in profile, walking left on a section of log.
history: Oregon is the only U.S. state whose flag has different designs on its two sides. The obverse carries the state shield, motto and date (see US-OR); the reverse carries the beaver alone. Both sides have been in use since adoption in 1925.

features.role: North American beaver (Castor canadensis), the state animal, walking on a log
features.symbolism: the beaver — Oregon's state animal, central to early Pacific Northwest fur trade and to the state's economic development; gives Oregon its nickname 'the Beaver State'
